user_message: [AUTOMATION TRIGGER] Daily automation: Calendar event with non-Microsoft external attendee detected. Account: Liberty's Delightful Sinful Bakery.

input_artifacts.content:
From: Patricia Kim <patricia.kim@libertysdeli.com>
To: Marcus Williams <marcus.williams@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-03-26 09:35 EST
Subject: Re: Re: QBR preparation Timeline

Our team encountered some challenges with QBR preparation. Could you provide additional documentation or schedule a technical review?

---
From: Marcus Williams <marcus.williams@microsoft.com>
To: Patricia Kim <patricia.kim@libertysdeli.com>
Date: 2024-02-12 12:03 EST
Subject: Follow-up: QBR preparation Review

Our team encountered some challenges with QBR preparation. Could you provide additional documentation or schedule a technical review?

---
From: Patricia Kim <patricia.kim@libertysdeli.com>
To: Marcus Williams <marcus.williams@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-01-24 09:12 EST
Subject: Follow-up: QBR preparation Review

Just checking in on the QBR preparation proposal. Our Director of IT is asking for a status update before the board meeting next week.

Meeting: QBR preparation Discussion - Liberty's Delightful Sinful Bakery
Date: 2024-02-03
Duration: 45 minutes
Attendees: Patricia Kim (Liberty's Delightful Sinful Bakery), Marcus Williams (Microsoft)

[00:01:30] Patricia Kim (Liberty's Delightful Sinful Bakery): Thanks everyone for joining. Let's dive into QBR preparation.

[00:08:00] Marcus Williams (Microsoft): Our director reviewed this and has concerns about integration complexity. Can we address those?

[00:11:00] Marcus Williams (Microsoft): Our architect reviewed this and has concerns about partner engagement. Can we address those?

[00:15:00] Patricia Kim (Liberty's Delightful Sinful Bakery): On the QBR preparation front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around performance requirements.

[00:17:00] Patricia Kim (Liberty's Delightful Sinful Bakery): Let me add some context -- we've been working on resource allocation and found that there's a compliance requirement we hadn't accounted for in the original scope.

[00:19:00] Marcus Williams (Microsoft): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is technical validation.

[00:21:00] Patricia Kim (Liberty's Delightful Sinful Bakery): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is security compliance.

Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF32-D failure mode (Invents attendee role when context is absent)
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct QBR preparation decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
